member.name: Chris Bryant
member.synopsis: Chris Bryant is the Labour MP for Rhondda and Ogmore , and has been an MP continually since 7 June 2001. He currently holds the Government post of Minister of State (Department for Business and Trade).

officialSourceNotes: Current member identity, party, constituency, thumbnail, contact information, synopsis, written questions, and registered interests are generated from the official UK Parliament Members API.
Recent Commons division positions are generated from the official UK Parliament Commons Votes API.
External official links are retained as citations; TOR Phi profile and record navigation remains internal.

writtenQuestions.heading: Trade Promotion: Saudi Arabia
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Minister for the Cabinet Office, how many Ministers attended the Great Futures conference in Riyadh, Saudi Arabia, on 14 and 15 May 2024.
writtenQuestions.answerText: 5 UK Ministers attended the conference.The GREAT FUTURES launch event in Riyadh on 14-15 May 2024 is the flagship moment that kickstarts a year-long programme bringing to life the sheer scale of opportunities for UK businesses in Saudi Arabia linked to Vi...
writtenQuestions.answeringBody: Cabinet Office

writtenQuestions.heading: Saudi Arabia: Trade Promotion
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Deputy Foreign Secretary, which human rights issues Ministers raised with their Saudi counterparts during the Great Futures conference in Riyadh, Saudi Arabia, on 14 and 15 May 2024.
writtenQuestions.answerText: UK ministers regularly raise human rights concerns with the Saudi authorities where appropriate. We will continue to raise human rights with the Saudi authorities through diplomatic channels, including via Ministers and the British Embassy in Riyadh.
writtenQuestions.answeringBody: Foreign, Commonwealth and Development Office

writtenQuestions.heading: Trade Promotion: Saudi Arabia
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Minister for the Cabinet Office, what the cost to the public purse is of the Great Futures programme in Saudi Arabia.
writtenQuestions.answerText: GREAT FUTURES showcases how UK expertise can help Saudi Arabia deliver against Vision 2030, their ambitious strategy to drive economic and societal change across the country. Including trade, investment, tourism, education and cultural engagement, GREAT F...
writtenQuestions.answeringBody: Cabinet Office

writtenQuestions.heading: Universal Service Obligation
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for Business and Trade, whether she has made an assessment of the potential impact on patients receiving letters from the NHS of changes to the universal service obligation.
writtenQuestions.answerText: Ofcom, as the independent regulator of postal services, is responsible for ensuring that the universal postal service meets the reasonable needs of customers as well as being financially sustainable and efficient. The Government does not have a role in Of...
writtenQuestions.answeringBody: Department for Business and Trade

writtenQuestions.heading: Broadband
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for Science, Innovation and Technology, how many premises were able to access a gigabit-capable broadband connection in each constituency in the period between March 2019 and March 2024.
writtenQuestions.answerText: Constituency-level statistics detailing the percentage of premises with access to a gigabit-capable connection over this period are available from the independent website Think Broadband.com. As of March 2024, over 81% of premises across the UK are able t...
writtenQuestions.answeringBody: Department for Science, Innovation and Technology

writtenQuestions.heading: Flowers: Diseases
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for Environment, Food and Rural Affairs, how many recorded incidents of disease were connected to imports from the EU of (a) gypsophila, (b) solidago, (c) orchids, (d) dianthus and (e) chrysanthemum between 2020 and 2023.
writtenQuestions.answerText: During 2020 – 2023 there were no findings of disease on cut flowers of the stated genera from the EU. Please note that import physical checks on cut flowers from the EU were not required during this time.
writtenQuestions.answeringBody: Department for Environment, Food and Rural Affairs

writtenQuestions.heading: Flowers: UK Trade with EU
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for Environment, Food and Rural Affairs, how many (a) gypsophila, (b) solidago, (c) orchids, (d) dianthus and (e) chrysanthemum consignments were imported from the EU between 2020 and 2023.
writtenQuestions.answerText: During 2020 – 2023 there were 54,432 imports of cut flowers of the stated genera from the EU.
writtenQuestions.answeringBody: Department for Environment, Food and Rural Affairs

writtenQuestions.heading: Flowers: Imports
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for Environment, Food and Rural Affairs, what was the total number of cut flower imports between 2010 and 2020.
writtenQuestions.answerText: During 2010 – 2020 there were 177,113 imports of cut flowers.
writtenQuestions.answeringBody: Department for Environment, Food and Rural Affairs

writtenQuestions.heading: Telemedicine
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for Science, Innovation and Technology, which stakeholders her Department has had discussions with on the Telecare National Action Plan.
writtenQuestions.answerText: The Department has engaged with key stakeholders on the Telecare National Action Plan, including the telecoms sector, telecare sector, the independent telecoms regulator Ofcom, the third sector – including Which?, Citizen's Advice, and Silver Voices - and...
writtenQuestions.answeringBody: Department for Science, Innovation and Technology

writtenQuestions.heading: Voice over Internet Protocol: Local Government
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for Science, Innovation and Technology, pursuant to the Answer of 1 May 2024 to Question 23701 on Voice over Internet Protocol: Local Government, which local authorities her Department has had discussions with; and on what da
writtenQuestions.answerText: The Department has previously engaged with Local Authorities on this issue via the Local Government Association. This engagement is ongoing and is in addition to frequent meetings with other relevant stakeholders, including industry and the signatories of...
writtenQuestions.answeringBody: Department for Science, Innovation and Technology

writtenQuestions.heading: Armed Forces: Uniforms
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for Defence, if he will make an assessment of the potential merits of manufacturing all British armed forces uniforms in the UK.
writtenQuestions.answerText: It has not proved possible to respond to the right hon. Member in the time available before Prorogation.
writtenQuestions.answeringBody: Ministry of Defence

writtenQuestions.heading: Armed Forces: Uniforms
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for Defence, if he will make an assessment of the potential impact of manufacturing armed forces uniforms abroad on security.
writtenQuestions.answerText: It has not proved possible to respond to the right hon. Member in the time available before Prorogation.
writtenQuestions.answeringBody: Ministry of Defence

writtenQuestions.heading: Armed Forces: Uniforms
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for Defence, if he will provide a breakdown of countries with current British Armed Forces uniform manufacturing contracts.
writtenQuestions.answerText: It has not proved possible to respond to the right hon. Member in the time available before Prorogation.
writtenQuestions.answeringBody: Ministry of Defence

writtenQuestions.heading: Armed Forces: Uniforms
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for Defence, what proportion of armed forces uniforms are manufactured in the UK.
writtenQuestions.answerText: It has not proved possible to respond to the right hon. Member in the time available before Prorogation.
writtenQuestions.answeringBody: Ministry of Defence

writtenQuestions.heading: Regulation: Public Consultation
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for Business and Trade, when she will publish a response to her Department’s consultation entitled Smarter regulation: strengthening the economic regulation of the energy, water and telecoms sectors, which closed on 28th Janu
writtenQuestions.answerText: The response to the consultation is expected to be published shortly as part of the Department’s Smarter Regulation programme.
writtenQuestions.answeringBody: Department for Business and Trade

writtenQuestions.heading: Food: Advertising
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for Health and Social Care, pursuant to the Answer of 23 April 2024 to Question 22526 on Food Advertising, what her planned timetable is for (a) publishing finalised guidance and (b) laying regulations before Parliament.
writtenQuestions.answerText: On 1 October 2025, the Government will introduce a United Kingdom-wide 9:00pm television watershed for the advertising of less healthy products, and a restriction of paid-for advertising of these products online. The Government has published and consulted...
writtenQuestions.answeringBody: Department of Health and Social Care

writtenQuestions.heading: Voice over Internet Protocol: Telemedicine
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for Science, Innovation and Technology, pursuant to the Answer of 22 April 2024 to Question 22261 on Voice Over Internet Protocol: Telemedicine, how many written updates from Communication Providers she has received on their
writtenQuestions.answerText: I can confirm that extensive discussions with Communication Providers (CPs) are ongoing, with a formalised reporting system having recently been established and a first update expected in May. Separately, Ofcom has also agreed a formal incident reporting ...
writtenQuestions.answeringBody: Department for Science, Innovation and Technology

writtenQuestions.heading: Voice over Internet Protocol
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for Science, Innovation and Technology, if she will publish a list of her Department's meetings on the public switched telephone network Charter since January 2024.
writtenQuestions.answerText: The Department has regular meetings with Local Authorities on this issue. That is in addition to frequent meetings with other relevant stakeholders, including industry and the signatories of the Charter.
writtenQuestions.answeringBody: Department for Science, Innovation and Technology

writtenQuestions.heading: Voice over Internet Protocol: Local Government
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for Science, Innovation and Technology, if she will publish a list of meetings her Department has had with local authorities on the switch off of the public switched telephone network since April 2023.
writtenQuestions.answerText: The Department has regular meetings with Local Authorities on this issue. That is in addition to frequent meetings with other relevant stakeholders, including industry and the signatories of the Charter.
writtenQuestions.answeringBody: Department for Science, Innovation and Technology

writtenQuestions.heading: Telemedicine: Voice over Internet Protocol
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for Health and Social Care, whether there are telecare devices being sold that will no longer be fully operational after the Public Switched Telephone Network is switched off.
writtenQuestions.answerText: It is a known risk that some analogue telecare devices may not be digitally compatible or perform as reliably on digital networks. In November 2021, the Technology Enabled Care Services Association (TSA), the industry and advisory body for technology enab...
writtenQuestions.answeringBody: Department of Health and Social Care
